vitals.terrain: plains, plateaus, steep-sided valleys, Ardennes hills, forests, cobblestones
vitals.aid_stations: assistance and refreshment points at life bases
vitals.participation_trend: growing as part of Race Across Series, registration open with multiple formats
vitals.location: Amsterdam to Arlon via Netherlands/Belgium/Luxembourg, with loops around Arlon

course_description.overview: Ultra-distance road cycling event starting in Amsterdam and crossing into Belgium and Luxembourg to Arlon, followed by a hilly loop around Arlon. Features flat northern plains transitioning to demanding Ardennes valleys, forests, and cultural sites across three countries.

key_segments.name: Part 1: Amsterdam to Arlon
key_segments.description: Cross-country from Dutch plains through Belgium, ~4,700 m elevation gain.

key_segments.name: Part 2: Arlon Loop
key_segments.description: Wilder, steeper loop through Ardennes valleys, Luxembourg borders, forests, and breweries, 7,165 m elevation gain.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Demanding ultra with over 11,900 m elevation, cobblestones, steep climbs, and long distances in a semi-autonomous format testing endurance across varied Benelux terrain.

logistics.getting_there: Start in Amsterdam (Netherlands); finish and base in Arlon, Belgium (Pl. Léopold). Shuttles available.
logistics.accommodation: Main logistical base in Arlon with rest areas, showers, dormitories, meals.
logistics.registration_details: Opens October 2025, closes May 2026. Entry fees from €109-€359 depending on distance and team format (solo/duo/team of 4). Part of Race Across Series.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road or gravel options; GPX files sent 30 days prior. Bike transport via shuttles.
logistics.local_considerations: Cross-border travel (Netherlands, Belgium, Luxembourg); weather variable in May; focus on beer culture, forests, and hilly regions.

final_verdict.one_liner: Epic ultra-road odyssey blending flat crossings, Ardennes climbs, and Benelux culture.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced ultra-cyclists seeking multi-country endurance challenges with elevation and history.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for its unique tri-national route and Race Across Series quality.
pro_tips: Train for Ardennes climbs and cobblestones.
Plan self-supported nutrition between life bases.
Embrace the beer and forest vibes post-race.
